title: Lankesteriana escalerensis

Further, Lankesteriana escalerensis is a member of the genus Lankesteriana, which is a small group of terrestrial orchids. The genus Lankesteriana is native to Central and South America. Lankesteriana escalerensis is specifically found in the mountainous regions of Costa Rica.

intro: Lankesteriana escalerensis, also known as Escaleria or Escalerian Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lankesteriana escalerensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also employed in traditional medicine to treat colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
